info.title: Elgiopay API Documentation
info.description: Accept mobile money, card, and bill payments across West and Central Africa.

tags.name: Webhooks
Testing
tags.description:
The sandbox replays deterministic outcomes — no real money moves and no real provider is contacted. Outcomes are driven by the `customer_phone` you send on `POST /payments` (or the `recipient_phone` you send on `POST /payouts`). Any phone not listed below falls back to a successful payment (`completed`, `error_code: 0`).

## Mobile money — phone-driven scenarios

Two number ranges are reserved by the simulator:

- `699000XXX` → Orange Money flavour
- `677000XXX` → MTN MoMo flavour

Behaviour is identical between the two ranges, so each row below applies to the matching `699000XXX` *and* `677000XXX` number.

### Success and pending-then-success

| Phone (Orange / MTN) | Outcome | After |
|---|---|---|
| `699000000` / `677000000` | `completed` | Immediate |
| `699000010` / `677000010` | `completed` | 10 seconds |
| `699000030` / `677000030` | `completed` | 30 seconds |
| `699000060` / `677000060` | `completed` | 1 minute |
| `699000120` / `677000120` | `completed` | 2 minutes |

The delayed numbers initially return `status: "pending"`. `GET /payments/{transaction_id}` keeps returning `pending` until the wall clock crosses the completion time, then flips to `completed`. Use them to verify your polling and webhook handling end-to-end.

### Failures

All failures return `status: "failed"` with a 4-digit `error_code` in the `9200` range so they're never confused with a real provider error.

| Phone (Orange / MTN) | `error_code` | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `699000201` / `677000201` | `9201` | Customer rejected the USSD/STK prompt |
| `699000202` / `677000202` | `9202` | Insufficient balance on the customer's wallet |
| `699000203` / `677000203` | `9203` | Customer did not confirm in time (timeout) |
| `699000204` / `677000204` | `9204` | Generic transaction failure |

### Default

Any phone number not in the tables above resolves to an immediate `completed` outcome. This mirrors MTN's own sandbox behaviour ("any other number → success").

### Payouts

The same tables apply to `POST /payouts`. Send the test number as `recipient_phone` — the simulator picks the matching outcome and `GET /payouts/{payout_id}` follows the same `pending` → `completed` / `failed` flow.

## Putting it together

1. Authenticate with a `pk_test_…` key against `https://sandbox-api.elgiopay.com`.
2. Issue `POST /payments` (or `POST /payouts`) using one of the test phone numbers above.
3. Poll `GET /payments/{transaction_id}` (or wait for your webhook) until the status reaches a terminal state.
4. Pair a delayed-success number (`699000060`, `677000120`, …) with a real webhook URL to validate your end-to-end pipeline before going live.

default.description: Generate your API keys from your app dashboard. Use pk_test_ keys for the sandbox and pk_live_ keys for production.

get.description: Returns your app's available, pending, and total balance for the requested currency.

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency code (XAF, XOF, EUR). Default: XAF.

schema.description: Currency code (XAF, XOF, EUR). Default: XAF.

get.description:
Returns the list of bill services your app can pay (electricity, water,
TV, etc.), optionally filtered by category. The service `code` returned
here is what you pass to `/bills/get-bill` and `/bills/pay`.

examples: Pay your ENEO postpaid electricity bill

examples.name: ENEO Postpaid
examples.description: Pay your ENEO postpaid electricity bill

services.name: ENEO Postpaid
services.description: Pay your ENEO postpaid electricity bill

post.description:
Fetch the outstanding bill amount and customer details for a given
service and account number. Call this before `/bills/pay` to show your
customer what they owe.

examples: The service code field is required.
The service code field is required.
The account number field is required.

examples.message: The service code field is required.

service_code: The service code field is required.
account_number: The account number field is required.

account_number.description: The customer's account/meter number with the service provider.

post.description:
Charge the customer and settle the bill with the service provider. The
customer will receive a mobile money prompt to authorize the charge.

examples: The amount must be at least 1.
The amount must be at least 1.
The customer phone field is required.

examples.message: The amount must be at least 1.

amount: The amount must be at least 1.
customer_phone: The customer phone field is required.

amount.description: Amount to pay in the service's currency.

customer_phone.description: Mobile money number that will be charged.

item_id.description: Optional payable item id returned by `/bills/get-bill`.

examples: API access is only allowed from api. or sandbox-api. subdomains

examples.message: API access is only allowed from api. or sandbox-api. subdomains

post.description:
Create a new payment transaction. The customer will receive a mobile money
prompt on their phone to authorize the charge.

examples: The selected payment method is invalid.

payment_method: The selected payment method is invalid.

customer_name.description: Must not be greater than 255 characters.

customer_email.description: Must be a valid email address. Must not be greater than 255 characters.

reference.description: Must not be greater than 255 characters.

get.description:
Returns the current status of a transaction. If the transaction is still
pending or processing, the system attempts a live verification with the
provider before responding.

examples: The specified transaction does not exist

examples.message: The specified transaction does not exist

parameters.name: transactionId
parameters.description: The transaction identifier returned when the payment was initiated.

post.description:
Force a live verification of the transaction against the payment provider
and update its status. Useful when a webhook was missed or you want to
confirm a payment before delivering goods.

examples: Payment was declined by the provider

examples.message: Payment was declined by the provider

get.description: Returns a paginated list of payouts created by your app, most recent first.

post.description:
Send funds from your available balance to a recipient. Requires sufficient
balance in the requested currency.

examples: The selected payout method is invalid.
The recipient name field is required.

payout_method: The selected payout method is invalid.
recipient_name: The recipient name field is required.

recipient_name.description:
'recipient_phone' => [
'required_if:payout_method,mtn_mobile_money,orange_money',
'string',
'regex:/^(\+237|237)?[67]\d{8}$/'
],. Must not be greater than 255 characters.

recipient_email.description: Must be a valid email address. Must not be greater than 255 characters.

bank_account_number.description: This field is required when payout_method is bank_transfer. Must not be greater than 50 characters.

bank_name.description: This field is required when payout_method is bank_transfer. Must not be greater than 100 characters.

bank_code.description: Must not be greater than 20 characters.

description.description: Must not be greater than 500 characters.
examples: Incidunt iure odit et et modi ipsum.

reference.description: Must not be greater than 255 characters.

get.description:
Returns the current status of a payout. Processing payouts are
automatically refreshed against the provider before responding.

post.description:
Check whether a phone number can receive a payout before creating one.
Returns the recipient's registered name if available.

recipient.description: The recipient's phone number (digits only, no country prefix).
